title: Unit 8: Doubly & Circular Linked Lists
description: Work with advanced linked list patterns where nodes point backward or loop in a circle.

topics.title: 8.1 Circular Linked List operations
topics.description: A Circular Linked List (CLL) is a sequence of nodes where the last node points back to the first node (head). Traversal requires stopping when returning to head.

complexity.time: O(1) insertion at head/tail with tail pointer, O(n) traversal

ispo.desc: Accepts value to insert.
Defines Node class structure.
Declares head node reference.
Allocates a new Node object.
Declares temp variable to walk list.
Handles inserting first element pointing to itself.
Finds last node and updates pointers to insert newNode at start, wrapping around.

quiz.q: What is the stopping condition when traversing a Circular Linked List to print elements?
o: current == head (after visiting at least once)

topics.title: 8.2 Doubly Linked List Node Structure
topics.description: Doubly Linked List (DLL) nodes contain previous and next pointers, allowing bidirectional traversal.

ispo.desc: Receives value to insert at front.
Declares DLLNode class variables data, prev, and next to represent DLL nodes in memory.
Declares head node pointer.
Allocates memory for a new DLLNode object.
Creates a new DLLNode, updates head.prev, sets next to head, and shifts head reference.

quiz.q: What is the main advantage of a Doubly Linked List over a Singly Linked List?
o: Permits backward traversal and O(1) deletes given a node pointer

topics.title: 8.3 Sorted Merge of Two Doubly Circular Lists
topics.description: Merge two sorted doubly circular linked lists into a single sorted doubly circular linked list.
topics.cheatSheet: Compare head values. Merge rest recursively. Set prev pointers. Link ends to form circle.

ispo.desc: Accepts head1 and head2 of both circular doubly lists.
Saves references to the last elements of both circular lists to break circle links.
Declares mergedHead reference variable to hold sorted linear merge result.
Declares current node pointer to traverse and locate end of list.
Breaks circles of both lists by setting last node next to null and head prev to null.
Locates the end of the merged list and links the last node back to head to restore circular properties.
Returns sorted merged circular doubly head.

quiz.q: Why is it helpful to break the circular connections temporarily before merging sorted circular DLLs?
o: To simplify the sorting merge logic by treating them as standard linear lists

topics.title: 8.4 Delete occurrences of Key in DLL
topics.description: Delete all nodes in a doubly linked list that contain a target key x.

ispo.desc: Takes DLL head and key x to remove.
Declares current pointer initialized to head.
Stores current's next node in nextNode prior to bypass.
Traverses DLL. If node value matches key x, rewrites neighboring nodes next/prev links to bypass and delete.
Returns updated head pointer.

quiz.q: What happens to pointers of neighbors when a node is deleted in a Doubly Linked List?
o: Only previous node's next pointer changes
Only next node's prev pointer changes
Both previous node's next pointer and next node's prev pointer are adjusted to bypass it

topics.title: 8.5 Delete Node at Position in DLL
topics.description: Delete a node located at a specific index position n (1-indexed) in a doubly linked list.
topics.cheatSheet: Move to pos. Link: curr.prev.next = curr.next; curr.next.prev = curr.prev;

ispo.desc: Accepts head node and 1-indexed target position to delete.
Declares current pointer walking variable.
Iterates until reaching the node at the specified position.
Deletes head if position is 1, updating head.prev and returning.
Updates next/prev pointers of node's neighbors to bypass the node in memory.
Returns updated head pointer.

quiz.q: What is the index of the first node when deleting at position in a standard 1-indexed function?
